name: Setanta
description: Setanta, later known as Cú Chulainn, is a legendary Irish hero renowned for his incredible strength, unparalleled battle prowess, and unwavering loyalty. Born as Setanta, his destiny was intertwined with prophecy from a young age. His early life was marked by exceptional feats of athleticism and bravery, foreshadowing his future as Ulster's greatest warrior. He famously earned the name Cú Chulainn (Hound of Culann) after accidentally killing Culann's fierce guard dog and offering to take its place until a replacement could be found. Cú Chulainn is best known for his heroic defense of Ulster against the army of Queen Medb of Connacht in the Táin Bó Cúailnge (Cattle Raid of Cooley). Single-handedly, he held off the invading forces, engaging in epic battles and demonstrating extraordinary resilience. His berserker rage, known as the warp spasm, transformed him into a terrifying force of nature. Despite his immense power, Cú Chulainn was also known for his honor, compassion, and tragic flaws. He was bound by geasa (taboos) which often led to difficult choices and ultimately contributed to his downfall. His death, a result of betrayal and the violation of his geasa, is one of the most poignant in Irish mythology, solidifying his status as a symbol of courage, sacrifice, and the tragic hero.
subtitle: The Hound of Ulster: Ireland's Greatest Hero
achievements: Defending Ulster in the Táin Bó Cúailnge
Defeating numerous warriors in single combat

keyMyths.title: The Naming of Cú Chulainn
keyMyths.description: As a boy, Setanta accidentally killed Culann's hound. To atone for his mistake, he offered to guard Culann's house until a replacement could be found, earning him the name Cú Chulainn, meaning 'Hound of Culann'. This act demonstrated his bravery and sense of responsibility.

keyMyths.title: The Táin Bó Cúailnge
keyMyths.description: Cú Chulainn single-handedly defended Ulster against Queen Medb's invading army. He invoked the right of single combat, engaging in numerous duels and showcasing his incredible strength and skill, holding off the enemy for months.

keyMyths.title: The Death of Cú Chulainn
keyMyths.description: Cú Chulainn's death was prophesied and came about due to a combination of his geasa and the treachery of his enemies. He was ultimately killed after being tricked into breaking his vows and facing overwhelming odds, dying standing upright, tied to a stone.

sources.author: Thomas Kinsella
sources.description: A modern translation of the Táin Bó Cúailnge, the central epic featuring Cú Chulainn.
sources.text: A modern translation of the Táin Bó Cúailnge, the central epic featuring Cú Chulainn.

sources.author: Gregory, Lady Augusta
sources.description: A retelling of the Cú Chulainn cycle of stories in accessible prose.
